zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor於1368年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾時，他的父親William Burton42歲，母親Matilda Curteis39歲。他和Lady Nancy Cranton至少有2個兒子。他在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾洛斯特被列為abbot of rocester。他去世於1461年3月29日在他的家鄉享年93歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor于1368年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾时，他的父亲William Burton42岁，母亲Matilda Curteis39岁。他和Lady Nancy Cranton至少有2个儿子。他在英国英格兰斯塔福爾洛斯特被列为abbot of rocester。他去世于1461年3月29日在他的家乡享年93岁。

nameMeanings.name: Burton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a placename that is very common in central and northern England. The derivation in most cases is from Old English burh ‘fort’ (see Burke ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
French (northeastern): regional variant of   Berton  .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Burtin
meanings: English: variant of   Burton  .
French (eastern): regional variant of   Bertin  .

variants.name: Borton
meanings: English: variant of   Burton  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Longnor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Langhaar, a nickname for a man with long hair. The surname Langhaar is virtually non-existing in Germany.
History: Johann Wilhelm Langhaar came to Wantage, NJ, in 1749 with his family from Rhineland, Germany.

variants.name: Longcore
meanings: Variant of   Longcor  , a surname of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor was born in 1368, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Burton, was 42 and his mother, Matilda Curteis, was 39. He had at least 2 sons with Lady Nancy Cranton. His occupation is listed as abbot of rocester in Rocester, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 29 March 1461, in his hometown, at the age of 93.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor est né en 1368, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Burton, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Matilda Curteis, avait 39 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Lady Nancy Cranton. Sa profession de son indiquée est abbot of rocester à Rocester, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 29 mars 1461, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 93 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor 1368 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Burton 42 und seine Mutter Matilda Curteis 39. Er hatte mit Lady Nancy Cranton mindestens 2 Söhne. sein Beruf ist als abbot of rocester in Rocester, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 29. März 1461 im Alter von 93 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor è nato il nel 1368 nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Burton, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Curteis, aveva 39 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Lady Nancy Cranton. La sua occupazione è indicata come abbot of rocester nel Rocester, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 29 marzo 1461, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 93 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnorが1368年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父William Burtonは42歳，母Matilda Curteisは39歳であった。彼はLady Nancy Crantonとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアロセスターでの職業はabbot of rocesterと記録されている。彼は1461年3月29日に故郷で93歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor(이)가 1368년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Burton(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Curteis(은)는 39세였다. 그는 Lady Nancy Cranton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 록스터에서 abbot of rocester이었다. 그는 1461년 3월 29일 그의 고향에서 93세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor nasceu em 1368, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Burton, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Curteis, tinha 39 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Lady Nancy Cranton. Sua profissão aparece como abbot of rocester em Rocester, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 29 de março de 1461, em sua cidade natal, com 93 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor родился в 1368 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Burton было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda Curteis, 39. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Lady Nancy Cranton.  Он работал по профессии abbot of rocester в местности росестир, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 29 марта 1461 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 93 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Walter Knight Burton of Longnor nació en 1368, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Burton, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Matilda Curteis, tenía 39 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Lady Nancy Cranton. Su ocupación registrada era abbot of rocester en Rocester, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 29 de marzo de 1461, en su pueblo, a la edad de 93 años.
